title: Shri Nirvana Shatakam
intro: Read Shri Nirvana Shatakam with Hindi meaning. Chant this profound hymn to seek self-realization and the grace of Lord Shiva.
description:
|| Shri Nirvana Shatakam ||

1. Mano buddhyahankara chittani naham, na cha shrotra jihve na cha ghrana netre.
Na cha vyoma bhumir na tejo na vayuh, Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham.||
2. Na cha prana sanjno na vai panchavayuh, na va saptadhatur na va panchakoshah.
Na vakpani padau na chopastha payu, Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham.||
3. Na me dvesha ragau na me lobha mohau, mado naiva me naiva matsarya bhavah.
Na dharmo na chartho na kamo na mokshah, Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham.||
4. Na punyam na papam na saukhyam na duhkham, na mantro na tirtham na vedah na yajnah.
Aham bhojanam naiva bhojyam na bhokta, Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham.||
5. Na me mrityushanka na me jatibhedo, pita naiva me naiva mata na janma.
Na bandhur na mitram gururneva shishyo, Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham.||
6. Aham nirvikalpo nirakara rupah, vibhurvyapya sarvatra sarvendriyanam.
Sada me samatvam na muktir na bandhah, Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham.||

|| Iti Shri Nirvana Shatakam Sampurnam ||

faq.a1: Nirvana Shatakam is a profound six-verse hymn composed by Adi Shankaracharya. It is a fundamental text of Advaita Vedanta, focusing on the true nature of the Self.
faq.q2: What is the central theme of this hymn?
faq.a2: The central theme is non-duality (Advaita). It negates everything that is not the Self (the body, the mind, the intellect, the senses) to assert that one is pure consciousness (Atman), which is synonymous with Shiva.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of reciting it?
faq.a3: Reciting this hymn helps detach from the ego and material identifications. It fosters deep self-inquiry, mental peace, and spiritual realization, eventually leading one to liberation from worldly suffering.
faq.q4: What does "Chidananda rupah Shivo'ham Shivo'ham" mean?
faq.a4: It means, "I am the form of pure consciousness and bliss; I am Shiva, I am Shiva." It is a powerful affirmation of one's divine nature.
